column7: I really appreciate QuickBooks Desktop Pro for handling bookkeeping and accounting efficiently. It provides easy and correct accounting, profit/loss statements, balance sheets, banking, and various other reports. One of the best features for me is the direct bank entries into the system, which saves a significant amount of time. With over 1000 bank entries each month, manually doing them would take 8-10 hours, whereas, with QuickBooks Desktop Pro, it brings it down to under 1 hour. Also, the initial setup was very easy, and I'd definitely recommend it to others.

Collaboration can be harder since it’s not cloud-native, and upgrades or license renewals can get expensive. It also requires manual backups and local installs, which adds extra maintenance.
column10: QuickBooks Desktop Pro helps manage core accounting tasks like invoicing, expense tracking, payroll, and financial reporting in one place. column7:
What works well:

Solid accounting basics: Handles bills, payments, reconciliations, and reports without issues.

Team use: Multiple users can work in the system, which helps with checks and reviews.

Widely known: Most staff and auditors are familiar with QuickBooks, so training is simple.

Reliable: The desktop version is stable and doesn’t depend on internet access.
column8:
What could be better:

Limited integrations: Doesn’t connect easily with newer tools like expense apps or CRM systems.

Outdated look: The design feels old-fashioned and isn’t as user-friendly as newer software.

Harder remote access: Since it’s desktop-based, working from different locations requires extra steps or add-on services.

Not ideal for growth: Great for small to mid-size groups, but bigger organizations may need more automation and advanced reporting.
column10:
QuickBooks Desktop Pro helps us manage the core of our accounting in one place — from paying vendors and tracking expenses to reconciling accounts and preparing financial statements. It gives us a reliable system for closing the books each month and producing reports for leadership and auditors.

The benefit is that we save time and reduce errors by having a consistent, structured process. Our finance team can quickly see where we stand, identify variances, and make adjustments before issues become bigger problems. Because so many people are already familiar with QuickBooks, it’s also easy to train new staff and work smoothly with auditors.

In short, QuickBooks Desktop Pro makes day-to-day accounting more efficient and gives us confidence in the accuracy of our financials.

column7: What I like best about QuickBooks Desktop Pro is its ability to offer powerful accounting tools without overwhelming the user. The software provides a solid foundation for managing finances, from tracking expenses to generating detailed reports, while maintaining a user-friendly interface. The offline access adds an extra layer of convenience and security, especially for businesses that prefer to keep their data local. Additionally, the customization options are a standout feature, allowing users to tailor reports and workflows to fit their unique needs. Overall, it strikes the perfect balance between simplicity and functionality.
column8: What I dislike about QuickBooks Desktop Pro is that its interface can feel a bit outdated, especially compared to newer, cloud-based alternatives. While the software is powerful, the learning curve can be steep for beginners who aren't familiar with accounting terms and concepts. The reliance on offline access may also be limiting for teams who need to collaborate in real-time or from multiple locations. Additionally, some users might find the pricing to be a bit high, especially when considering the cost of annual updates and support.
column10: QuickBooks Desktop Pro solves several key problems, particularly in streamlining the accounting and bookkeeping processes for small to mid-sized businesses. It helps me stay organized by efficiently tracking expenses, generating accurate financial reports, and managing payroll—all in one place. The software automates many tasks, reducing the risk of human error and saving valuable time. For me, it's been incredibly beneficial by providing real-time insights into financial health, making tax season much less stressful, and improving the overall financial decision-making process for my business. Powerful

column7: What I like best about QuickBooks Desktop Pro is its robustness and reliability when it comes to handling complex accounting tasks. The software offers comprehensive features like journal entries, customized reporting, multi-user access, and detailed job costing, which are especially useful for small to mid-sized businesses. The interface, while not flashy, is highly functional and allows for deep customization. I also appreciate the ability to work offline and maintain full control over my financial data without relying on a cloud connection.
column8: What I dislike about QuickBooks Desktop Pro is that the user interface feels outdated and not very intuitive, especially for new users. The learning curve can be steep without prior accounting knowledge, and some processes—like setting up customized reports or importing data—can be a bit clunky. Additionally, the lack of seamless integration with modern third-party apps and cloud platforms makes it less flexible compared to cloud-based alternatives like QuickBooks Online. Also, the fact that it's tied to a specific machine unless hosted remotely can be limiting for teams working in hybrid or remote environments.
column10: QuickBooks Desktop Pro helps streamline and centralize all of my accounting tasks, which saves time and reduces the risk of errors. It solves problems like tracking income and expenses, reconciling bank statements, managing payroll, generating detailed financial reports, and maintaining accurate records for tax filing. For a small business or consultancy, this level of organization is crucial. It allows me to make informed financial decisions, stay compliant, and focus more on growing the business rather than getting buried in spreadsheets. The offline access also ensures I can work securely without being dependent on internet connectivity. QuickBooks Desktop Pro: A Reliable Accounting Workhorse for

column7: QuickBooks Desktop Pro is a solid choice to help control the financial aspects of your new business. It has a broad user base, and many people have used this software. So, if you are a small growing business, you can easily find staff well-versed to take advantage of QBooks capabilities. Its features also make it easy to converse crucial financial information to your CPA. It has a lot of flexibility: export reports and data into Excel or a PDF.
column8: The one thing I don't like about QuickBooks is that entries can be modified. Most accounting systems have activities permanently posted, meaning anything processed in the software, a record is kept-basically an audit trail. So as your business grows and more people are involved in accounting you'll likely want a software that has this feature to ensure a valid audit trail.
column10: We're in construction services (electrical work) and like the job costing features, specifically using Job Class to assign different projects. It gives a lot of visibility into how each specific job performs financially. Many of the canned reports make generating a financial package easy and instantaneous. QuickBooks

There are many things I like about Quickbooks Desktop Pro. A few of them would be the fact that multiple users can log in with restricted and verified access by the main user preventing security breaches. The fact that creditors , debtors , invoices, bills , payment received and every aspect relating to the financial aspect is taken care of. The stock , mark ups , can also be entered, adjusted and much more. There are many perks as Quickbooks also has access for the accountant to do journal entries and run reports based on each department basically in the supply chain to run your firm effectively and efficiently with order being maintained l.
Quickbooks can integrate with Microsoft to easily import and export files. It has many great features and analysis that is needed showing profitability and so on. Statements can be sent directly to clients and payments received. There is another to learn on QB Desktop Pro which would would allow you to run your firm at optimum levels.
column8: It is a bit different and difficult to use for the average person but any great advanced software is and needs to be learnt and there is sufficient support for this. I don't dislike anything regarding Quickbooks Desktop Pro as it works well in big or small firms. I have used this software and implemented it and can definitely say it is one of the best accounting , ERP softwares available.
column10: It solves all my problems regarding my debtors and creditors and sales , it takes care of tax or vat which is an integral part in any business. The reporting allows me to conduct financial analysis and make decisions based in them. The stock and mark ups are well taken care of allowing me to have such leverage would reduce my overheads. QUICKBOOKS DESKTOP PRO IS ONE OF THE BEST

column7: QuickBooks Desktop Pro easy-to-use accounting software that keeps my bookkeeping accurate and up to date with simple data entry. Pre-designed report templates make review and information gathering readily available and easy to access. Payroll is so simple, and even paying and filing payroll taxes only takes a few clicks. Job costing is almost automatic, and the reports make it very quick and simple to see how much profit any given job has yielded. It is also easy to make an accountant's copy to send over to our tax preparer at the end of the year so I don't have to send multiple reports and documents. The tax preparer simply receives the accountant's copy, utilizes the information to prepare our tax returns, makes any necessary entries and adjustments, and then returns it to me to download into my system. Business life made easy!
column8: I tried very hard to think of a downside to QuickBooks Desktop Pro, but nothing comes to mind. The one and only thing I could complain about at all would possibly be that their tech support team is not easy to reach.

column7: The most helpful feature of QuickBooks Desktop Pro is that it is very flexible. It is easy to configure invoicing, banking, expenses, employees, reports and taxes in QuickBooks. However, the most appreciable quality of QuickBooks is that, unlike other ERPs, it is very easy to implement. Maintaining QuickBooks is easy and doesn't require a dedicated support team. One best thing about QuickBooks Desktop Pro is that it is moderately priced, and its pricing plans suit startups and small-sized enterprises. To get such a robust accounting engine at that price point is impossible. Compared to its competitors, QuickBooks offer better accounting reports, product support, and system stability.
column8: The one thing that QuickBooks finds challenging to handle is the high volume of master data. QuickBooks works perfectly with fewer suppliers, customers and transactional data. While QuickBooks realise most bookkeeping features, it doesn't offer many third-party integration opportunities. One scope for improvement in QuickBooks is that it can provide workflow customisations.
column10: QuickBooks is at the core of our accounting process. The entire R2R record-to-report process is done in QuickBooks. The benefits we have reaped from utilising QuickBooks are numerous, our accounting software licensing costs have been reduced, and the cash flow visibility has increased many folds. QuickBooks Desktop Pro is a cloud-based accounting engine, and it can be accessed from anywhere, anytime, from any device, which has boosted productivity. QuickBooks Desktop Pro is a powerful software that accurately tracks income, expenses, and payments and has helped increase our business performance with its speed and accuracy. Robust cloud Accounting solution suited for small and medium enterprises - QuickBooks

column7: <v ->My name is Pankaj.</v> I belong to information technology industry. I am heading finance and accounting function at the IT company. And I would like to rate QuickBooks as nine out of 10. Presently, we are using QuickBooks for doing our day to day routine accounting. The software offers features where you can upload the invoices and other supporting documents along with the transaction that you make. It also has a master data feature where you can upload your customer master data or, and your supplier master data. The QuickBooks function offers a facility where in the bank transactions can be uploaded very easily through converting a Excel, Excel download of your bank statement into a CSV file. And you can, before importing it into the software, you can review the transaction. The dashboard provided by this software is amazing and very appealing. You can turn on an opta privacy, if you do not wish to see the numbers on the dashboard itself. The reporting section of the software offers a wide range of reports, which are, which are sufficient to cater to the need of the local management. Most of the weekly and daily reports, which the managers review are available, like account receivable reports, supply balance report, list balance, and as well as the detail one. The other accounting reports such as balance sheet, P and L, trial balance are available on a click of a button. You can customize the columns in the report. Also the software offers, offers a feature of audit trail since you can go and check the transactions along with the supporting documents. The best part of the software, which I personally like the most is the reporting section and the facility wherein we can upload the documents next to the transactions. The reporting section helps you to get the reports like your receivable report, payable report, and similar other reports like balance sheet and the P and L on a click of a button. You can also customize these reports. The accounting function like doing the accounting in the software is very smooth and the learning co-op is very less. like you, you can learn this software in a very short span of time and you can be very productive in it. Actually, the software is perfect, but yes, they can come up, I think if QuickBooks can come up with a system where in now we can go through multiple tabs with keyboard itself, like we don't have to use mouse again and again, it will improve the productivity. The rest I think the system is very well designed for the small and medium enterprises. I will recommend QuickBooks, especially to the small and the medium enterprises. The software is very well designed and it's sufficient to cater the needs, starting from accounting to financial management. As mentioned earlier, the reporting structure is very good in the software. Also for the users to input the data. It is easy to understand and you can quickly learn. Also the software can be deployed to your teams very easily. The cost of the software as compared to the ERPs in my opinion, is reasonable.

column7: I have been using QuickBooks for several years now, as have many of my clients and my accountant. The popularity of QuickBooks means that when I have a question about how to do something, I am sure to find someone who knows the answer. QuickBooks is designed with an interface that makes it fairly easy to figure out how to do the basic functions. It's is very reliable. I can't remember it ever crashing, or leaving the database damaged because of some error.
column8: QuickBooks is a complex, sophisticated piece of software. As a result, there is definitely a steep learning curve to really get good with QuickBooks. Although the basics are straight forward and easy to figure out, anything beyond that seems to get complicated very quickly. Finding things in menus, figuring out how to produce reports, etc., all seem to take time and effort to figure out. Of course, part of the problem is that I am not an accountant, and trying to figure things out, without knowing how to be an accountant, probably makes it all a lot more difficult.
column10: The problems I am solving are that I need to be able to keep track of the financial aspects of running a business. I have to send invoices to my clients. I have to record payments from my clients. I have to keep track of my expenses. I have to balance my checkbook. I have to be able to produce the information needed to do my taxes. QuickBooks does all these things for me efficiently and reliably. I can't imagine how I would do this without QuickBooks.

column7: I've been using Quickbooks for my content marketing business for the past 3 years. It's intuitive, easy to understand, and helps me stay on top of my business income, expenses, and estimated taxes. I really appreciate the fact that it estimates my quarterly tax payments. Saves me a lot of time every quarter. It's very simple to categorize my expenses. And I love the invoicing feature. I can also upload all my information straight to my tax software, which is a huge plus come tax season.
column8: A couple downsides: the estimated tax payments tend to skew too big every quarter. I appreciate the fact that they are not too small, but I wish they were more accurate. I also recently ran into an issue where I was locked out of my QB account for no reason and had to pay for a subscription to get back in. That was a pain.

column7: Hi, I'm Elizabeth. I'm a business owner. I am a bookkeeper and I give QuickBooks pro and nine out of 10. I used QuickBooks desktop pro to serve my clients. The problems I saw are their monthly financial reporting. I prepare their bank reconciliations. Credit card reconciliations enter their income and expenses. It is a much faster way of getting this work done, then previous versions of QuickBooks and also not using QuickBooks at all. What I like best about QuickBooks desktop pro are the built in features that help be process information quickly. One of the features is the automatic numbering in different parts of the program. You can even put a plus cider and a negative sign to move a number up, up or back. Um, you can also have use automated automated bank feeds. Thio bring in payments from a bank account, and then you just have to classify them instead of having to manually enter each one. What I think could be improved with QuickBooks Desktop Pro is the functionality of reports when they are exported to excel. If you make any changes to the document, the spreadsheet, the, uh, the formulas that are embedded, Um screw up the totals and things get a little fuzzy, and it's very difficult to modify. A report once has been exported without making global changes. Part of the problem is that, uh, the data comes through with words and numbers on two lines going across it rose instead of on one road. So if you want to modify report, you have to manually select every other line to make changes. If you're considering QuickBooks Desktop Pro, I would recommend the product over the online version of the software. The reason is the desktop version is stable and you don't pay monthly. You pay annually and you don't have to upgrade every single year. If you use the online version, you have to pay monthly. And, uh, you know it's a little more difficult to export the data and have it sync up with, um, the desktop version of the software. Uh, the other reason is that the online version is only as good as the Internet that you have when you have a dust top version, it's downloaded to your computer so you don't have to have the Internet to do work in QuickBooks, and as a result, it's more stable. You know, sometimes the product crashes. If you have multiple people using it online, you don't have an instant update.

I love how easy Quickbooks is to customize and integrate with other softwares. As the defacto accounting software pretty much all apps integrate with it which makes the workflow so much easier. 

The other thing I love about QB is that transactions can be changed after reconciliation (all while keeping an audit log) So if someone miscategorizes something it can be edited later, unlike other softwares I have used where once you have reconciled no edits can be made (not even to memo or chart of accounts. =)
column8:
I don't like that if you have multiple companies you can only pull selected reports across all of them. I wish there was more integrations between companies, such as vendor information and chart of accounts. (You can sometimes work around this by Classing transactions for different companies, but certain transactions such as A/R and transfers cannot be effectively classed)

As basically the standard in Accounting I wish that when you were sent an invoice in QB from QB that you could automatically upload the information into the receiving company's file. (obviously then changing the chart of accounts) it would save so much time!

column7: <v ->Hi, my name is Latoya.</v> I'm an accountant, and I rate Quick Books Pro a seven out of 10. Quick Books Desktop Pro is a software in which allows you to provide financial statements to clients. And we are using the software to do just that. It helps to just organize clients' books better. What I like best about Quick Books Desktop Pro is that is has some back door features that you don't necessarily have to use Quick Books the way it is designed to be used. Such as you can enter things by the month and not necessarily by the day, and still get the end result that you're looking for when it comes to keeping clients' books. What I dislike about Quick Books Desktop Pro is that the software tends to always come out with a newer version. Which means that certain features in the older version are discontinued. So it's kind of like you think you're buying a software and you're gonna be good to go. But you're gonna be required, if you wanna use certain features of the software, you have to purchase that newer version. If you are considering Quick Books Desktop Pro, then there are a few things that you have to remember, which is that it's gonna be limited to only a desktop version. And I have experienced that you can only run Quick Books Desktop Pro using Windows. It works best with a Windows product. However, my recommendation of a software to use would be Quick Books Online. But your client or yourself would have to be willing to pay a monthly fee in order to do so. But it's just easier to go online, because you get to access your client's books across all types of devices, and on the go. However, if you are looking to just pay that one time fee for a software, Quick Books Desktop

I downloaded the product and tried to install it as I have with previous versions over the last 20 years. When finished, QuickBooks was no where to be found, specifically the ".exe" file. After three attempts I contacted Intuit. What I found out was that they are aware of the flaw. Download a trial version of 2012 and covert the data to 2012, then 2019 should be able to convert your 2012 data into 2019.
I cant confirm it, but I was from Quickbooks 2007, jumped to 2017 using trial version to update my data. 
I hope my experience would help you.

column8:
First and foremost, I greatly dislike Quickbook's support. They have no idea how to solve some of the basic issues with their software. When I upgraded from 2015 to 2019, my quickbooks file did not convert and I must have spent 2 hours on the phone trying to figure it out with support with no solution. If I wanted more help, I had to pay for it. I had to recreate the entire thing from scratch. 

Other thing I don't like is the vendor editing. There are times when I want to change or delete vendors, but even if I remove all transactions with that vendor from the system, it only allows me to make that vendor inactive.

Finally, it would be great if all the tax codes were pre-assigned rather than you having to choose them. As a new business owner, I did not know which codes work with which expenses, so there was definitely a learning curve.

column7: Extremely easy to learn. I use Quickbooks every single day. Some days are for payroll, time cards, payables/receivables, and/or taxes. column8: Inventory Issues - We store a lot of sheet metal and other items. No matter what we do, every year the counts are way off. We have worked many hours on fixing this problem, it is getting better, but still a work in progress! Seriously, this is the only issue we have had.
column9: We went from doing multiple entries in our books, to just doing minimal work daily on Quickbooks! Payroll is 10 times easier and quicker. Taxes are a breeze to complete every year! Literally just a click of a button and there is all the information there for you to print out and submit!
column10: Inventory is the only issues I have had with QuickBooks, so be sure to start that off on the correct foot from the get go! It can get away from you so easily.

column7:
Quickbooks is the gorilla of the accounting industry! I owned 2 businesses, and using Quickbooks to manage my accounting and invoicing needs was the best decision I made for organizing my records. 
What I liked best regarding Quickbooks, was the ability to send invoices directly from the software. That feature was very helpful since I'd often have to send dozens of invoices each day.
column8: I did not have any dislikes regarding Quickbooks. The software was 100% reliable, and I have nothing to dislike about it.
column9:
The problems I was solving when running my business regarding Quickbooks was in regards to budgeting. Quickbooks allows you to view a quick report on any item / account you need. it will bring up all the transactions during a given time period, and you can see what the income and expenses look like for a particular item/account. This was a tremendous advantage for my business. 

The benefits I realized is that it helps to be organized in quickbooks. I was extremely organized with my items, and all I needed to do at the end of the year was hand over my quickbooks to my accountant, and he would take it from there.
column10: Be patient with this software if you are new to it. You may need to take a course online in order to fully understand the ability Quickbooks has to offer. It has quite the learning curve but it certainly is worth every penny if you know what you're doing in it.

column7:
The user interface flexible and customizable--features are available to please the most visual users, but they can easily be closed to suit those of us who are distracted by flashy GUIs and just want to interact with the database.

Menus and features are not weighted by what Intuit thinks the user will want to see, as they are in QBO. This means that it is easier in QB Desktop to find the functionality that you need. It also makes QB Desktop a better tool for beginners, and for teaching beginners.

The Accountant Tools (especially bulk editing of transactions) are great, and more fully featured than they are in QBO. There are a few tweaks that I would make, but I really appreciate QB Desktop's Accountant Tools.

QB Desktop makes it generally easy to tag data on multiple dimensions, which makes it flexible--suitable for accounting and reporting needs from very simple to quite complicated.

If your tagging needs are very complicated, you may be better off doing data entry outside the program and importing it. Luckily, QB Desktop does not limit the kinds of transactions you can import, and the data's underlying mark-up (IIF) is publicly well documented, open to all users, and fairly easy to learn and use. (All of these are advantages over QBO.)

QB Desktop's Advanced Search feature is one of my favorite things. It allows you to search on dozens of parameters without first narrowing what kind of transaction you wish to search. The flexibility of such a search is obviously valuable, and it also supports multiple use styles; you don't have to think about how another user might have entered a transaction, or who that user might have been. You just enter your parameter, and the database turns up everything that fits. (For this reason, plus a few other minor restrictions that QBO imposes on searches, the search capabilities of QB Desktop are clearly superior to those of QBO.)

The program is rarely slow to respond or laggy.
column8:
Multiuser access is costly. Online access requires hosting on an application server, which can be expensive; it can also be complicated, which is a turn-off to unsupported/casual users.

The bank-transaction import/matching feature is a little lacking; it doesn't learn rules quickly and isn't very flexible. If you're quick at data entry and/or your data tagging needs are complicated, you may just decide never to use it.

Repairing bank reconciliations that have been thrown off by changes or deletions is more difficult than it is in QBO. Would love to see the QBO feature that shows you what went wrong and offers you a way to fix it without undoing and redoing everything implemented here.

The audit trail is a little less functional in QB Desktop than it is in QBO.

Lacks a real inventory (costing) system, though third-party solutions are available.

Lacks the ability to store percentages by which costs need to be allocated; this means that you're better off doing complicated allocation data entry outside the program and then importing it; it also hobbles the program for allocated budgeting and forecasting.

Lacks safeguards against importing the same data multiple times.

From a database perspective, the Name field is poorly defined, which can be a problem if you need to report on multiple dimensions (I've run into this problem particularly with non-profits that need very detailed reporting).

When QB Desktop crashes, it crashes hard, but support is generally very good.
column9:
QB Desktop is an all-around solid piece of accounting software.

Part of my business is teaching clients to work on and understand their own books. QB Desktop is great for this, especially when compared to QBO. (QBO's design and wording favor cash accounting, which is not appropriate for most businesses, but which is intuitive to those with no bookkeeping knowledge; this makes it a difficult tool to use to teach beginners.)
column10:
QB Desktop suits a wide range of bookkeeping needs, from simple to relatively complicated, and it is very flexible and customizable.

It also allows you to implement DIY solutions, especially to data tagging, manipulation, and import needs, without the need to rely on (and pay) third-party vendors.

If you have little to no bookkeeping knowledge, or are training/working with someone who lacks that knowledge, I far prefer QB Desktop to QBO.

QB Desktop is worth the trouble and cost of licensing multiple users and using an application server for online access.

column7: Quickbooks has easy to use features such as the home screen which provides a helpful flow chart of business processes. Each icon on the chart can be selected and the user can input their data immediately. For instance, if I need to pay a bill I will select the "Bills" icon. When I am ready to pay the bill I will select the "Pay Bill" icon.
column8: Quick books is customization to a user's business preferences and needs, however the downside is if the users lack accounting knowledge. A good example of this is when a user makes a payment on a loan. column7: Quickbooks is easy to use and simple to set up because they have several templates to get you started. I also like the reporting feature where you can see your current situation, see cash flow, understand how you've performed against last year... all at a glance.
column8: Sometimes I find that Quickbooks has so many options to decide, it can be cumbersome to set up and or proceed. column8:
PAYPAL DUPLICATE TRANSACTIONS: I couldn't figure out why I had up to three transactions in Quickbooks for every one PayPal debit card transaction. Frequently, there would be two debits and one credit in Quickbooks. It turns out that there is no workaround to prevent this from happening, even in the new software I'm using, but the new software clearly showed me what was happening with my account, which made finding a solution easy. (I'm currently in the process of switching accounting software.)

MISTAKES: Quickbooks also does not allow you to easily undo mistakes. Sometimes, you simply cannot. :-/

MOBILE APP: I get an error every time I try to upload a picture of a receipt using the mobile app. This is why I dinged "reliability." 

RESOLUTION: With the PayPal issue, Quickbooks required that I unlink PayPal and connect it a certain way. This deleted all of my work with my previous transactions, such as categorizing and uploading receipts. 

NOT FOR BEGINNERS: As someone who catches on quickly, QuickBooks was NOT intuitive (unlike the new software I'm switching to). Even after figuring out my PayPal issue, QB seems like too much of a pain to continue with.

column7: We use QB Desktop Pro for EVERYTHING! Paychecks, Invoicing, Customer Sales, Human Resources. The user interface is very simple. We have everyone in the office using it, and love being able to customize who can see what, and who has authorizations to perform certain tasks. We have also gotten a ton of use out of the Desktop File Doctor and the QB Connection Diagnostic Tool. We recently replaced our main computer and it threw us for a loop the first few days. Using those two tools, however, we were able to get our systems back up and running. Our staff usually receives very limited training, making this the PERFECT system. It requires very little explanation, and can be picked up over the course of a day, and mastered in less than a month.
column8: Unless you take advantage of all of it's features, QB Desktop Pro can be somewhat clunky. I've read similar reviews that critique the ease of creating and sending invoices, and I agree. column8:
Generating and sending invoices is more convoluted than it has any reason to be. There is no way to easily re-bill a set of invoices, you have to go into each one and select it. Sometimes when you create a new invoice for a client, it doesn't mark it as ready to print when saved, and there is no default settings to change this behavior. This has been a known issue for several years and Intuit doesn't seem to care about changing it.

Reports are pretty good. The ability to drill down on a report is better than just about any other program I've used. You can click on almost any number on a report and get a sub-report of everything that comprises that number.

Bank feeds cut off about half of the incoming transaction description from the bank (the rest gets put in memo field), extending the import would make creating rules a lot more useful.

column7: Quickbooks makes it easy to track expenses against a budget, keep up with payroll, or track down payments. It also ensures that you are always on top of things when it comes time to make tax withholding payments. It keeps all of your financial information and accounting records in one place. It's also helpful in tracking accruals of vacation time.
column8: Sometimes the search function isn't the easiest to work with. When trying to find a missing payment or a specific dollar amount, it sometimes doesn't seem to return all of the matching transactions. There is a little bit of a learning curve to start using Quickbooks.
column9: Quickbooks gives us an easy way to keep track of personnel payroll, vacation accruals, tax information, and custom deductions. It makes it easy to make sure we are following all state and federal guidelines on withholding as well. This is in addition to all of the other business benefits of tracking checks, revenue, expenses, etc.
column10: Quickbooks is the standard in accounting software, so I can't recommend this enough. Even if you aren't entering transactions on a daily basis, it's still helpful for an executive to be familiar enough to look up information so that you don't always have to rely on your accountant or bookkeeper to pull the information you need. The only drawback is that the one time we did need support, there were long waits on the phone and it was a little difficult to get our problem solved.

column7:
I use Quickbooks for Mac 2016 to track finances for eight LLC’s that contain real estate investment property. Each entity must be treated as a separate company in QB for bookkeeping and tax purposes.

I have used the product for years with general success. It has broad functionality, but the Mac version sometimes has bugs.
column8:
Intuit has announced the discontinuation of support for the desktop version for the mac starting in May of 2019.

Converting to QB online would require me to purchase eight separate subscriptions. My accounting methodology utilizes the of ‘classes’ feature in QB, therefore requiring me to subscribe to the PLUS version. Total cost would be $50 x 8 companies x 12 months = $4800 annually.

When I expressed concern to Intuit, they offered the following options:

1. Get rid of my Mac and buy a PC
2. Stop upgrading my Mac from OS and stay with OS High Sierra forever

I see only two solutions that Intuit can offer to avoid alienating a broad section of the Mac community:

1. Reverse this new policy and continue upgrading and supporting QB for Mac
2. Offer a commercial solution by offering deep package discount for users like me would need multiple subscriptions of QB online.

column7: Quickbooks 2015 Pro is what I currently have as our accounting software and I currently have 5 businesses and/or organizations accounts that I use under the one license key that I purchased in 2015. I have been using Quickbooks Pro for many years, but only purchase a new version about every 4 to 5 years due to the high cost of the software application. Quickbooks is very easy and user friendly with great support by using the "HELP" menu. You do not need to have a CPA college degree to use the Quickbooks software as there are tips that will walk you through the software application until you are completely comfortable in shutting the tips options off. You can create many accounts under one license. So, with someone like me who is a treasurer for several organizations and/or clubs as well as being a small business owner, it is very cost effective due to the purchase price of the actual Quickbooks Accounting software application.
column8: The dislike that I have about the Quickbooks software application is the price of the software. It is very hard for someone like me who is a non-paid secretary/treasurer of many organizations and/or clubs to keep the software up-to-date. Another option that is available is to be able to email customers the invoices directly from the Quickbooks application, but for some reason my software application will not allow me to do so. So, I have to create invoice, receipts, and statements in the Quickbooks software, save down as a PDF using the CutiePDF application, and then sending the forms to the customers using my gmail accounts.
column9: By using the Quickbooks Accounting software, I am able to keep an account for each of the organizations and clubs that I belong to as well as my home-based businesses all in one place. So, that is a wonderful business solution for me. This is also the best benefit of the Quickbooks Accounting software.
column10: Please keep in mind that unless you are a larger business owner, Quickbooks is not always cost effective to keep purchasing and updating every year.

column7:
We've used quickbooks for about 10 years and every year there are small changes that make it a little more pleasurable to use and a little easier to use. Over the years haven't seen a major change that will require all of us to re-learn so the additions and changes do not get into they way when we are trying to work.

The forms editor is quite good (not perfect); it is easy to enter basic information and payroll is pretty easy to use.
column8:
The software crashes a lot. Sometimes it just refuses to start, sometimes it kicks out people "just because" and sometimes it is impossible to get back in until the server or the user's computer is restarted. 

When quickbooks is installed, a lot of additional unwelcome software is installed on my machine that runs even when I am not using quickbooks at all, that is very annoying.

Saving to PDF and emailing from QuickBooks (if you use Outlook) can be challenging and tends to lock up a lot. We use Adobe's "PDF printer" to generate PDFs and never use Quickbooks' Save as PDF function unless we are in a suicidal mood.

Their support people is nice but extremely pushy whenever we call for something, always trying to sell something new and extra. Very annoying. Last couple of years they are a bit less pushy but still very annoying sometimes. 

Lastly, renewing payroll if you want to purchase the renewal through a third party like Amazon or Office Depot or whatever is a labor of patience and love. Intuit will always try to charge you for the payroll update (last time they charged us like $ 400) and then later refund. It always entails calling tech support with the serial # one purchased and well, waste 2-3 hours on the ordeal. This happens every year.

So, the software is good and useful but it drives one crazy.
column9: We handle all accounting and payroll with it. We have electronic deposit for employees and e-checks for suppliers so all in all, very helpful. Very complete +/- easy to use... But if it fails, good luck fixing it.
column10:
Be sure to have someone technically savvy at hand because eventually it will break and you will panic (very justifiably).

Accounting is at the heart of a company's operation so if you are unable to access your data, well, it can be disaster.

column7:
DISCLAIMER: My review is based on my experience with other accounting software like Xero (recently used), QBO (recently used), MSGP (back in 2014), Intaact (2012), ACS Technologies (2009) and BPCS (2009). 

The QuickBooks Desktop product line (e.g. Pro, Premier, Enterprise, Accountant, etc.) is my number one favorite accounting software. Relative to other accounting software, some reasons why QuickBooks is #1 in my opinion are:

1) It's intuitive (which makes the owning softwar company's name, Intuit, very fitting). You can find what you are looking for easily with its menus or online community portal.
2) Unlike other Frankenstein software like MSGP, QuickBooks desktop is put together from beginning to end in mind. MSGP by comparison is a mess because in order to satisfy what the company needs for accounting, the user has to incorporate a bunch of add-ons. The whole conversation process costs a fortune and the contractors you hire to make the switch seem to NEVER finish the implementation. It's just a huge mess with good money thrown after bad. 
3) You'll find your answers. QuickBooks is ubiquitous among businesses small and large. If you can't figure out what you need yourself, go to youtube or google and ask your question. It's rare that I don't find an answer to what I need this way. 
4) It's fast. QuickBooks can move as fast as you can hit the keystrokes. Their shortcut keys make for higher productivity. Reports can be modified in seconds. You can find stuff in no time. 
5) There's a audit trail report.
column8:
There's not much I dislike about QuickBooks desktop. Maybe a few things are:
1) I cannot have more than one A/R or A/P line item in a transaction. 
2) Aside from manual journal entries, I wish that QuickBooks would default the debit line item first before credits after a transaction is entered.
3) There are plenty of controls, but it might be nice to have it so that a staff accountant can input the transaction, but the manager has to approve it.

Even though QuickBooks is user friendly by comparison, if you don't understand accounting, there's a very good chance you'll screw up your books. That's no fault of QuickBooks - it's user error. Maybe this is why QuickBooks has a stigma with accountants in larger companies - "QuickBooks is for neophytes, not sophisticated financial professionals like myself," they say. 

QuickBooks has the reputation for only being suitable for mom-and-pop companies, not larger companies with employees. I've had accounting supervisors tell me this multiple times at different companies. When I press them for specific reasons why QuickBooks does not work for their company, many times they cannot think of a reason why - it's just the fashionable thing to say. 

There may be some legitimate reasons why a company needs to upgrade from QuickBooks Pro. For example, you cannot consolidate financial statements from multiple companies (but QuickBooks Enterprise can). Or if you need a full blown ERP.

But before you make the switch ask yourself: DO THE BENEFITS OUTWEIGH THE COSTS!! If you switch to a Frankenstein software (like MSGP), you might get new functionality and integration from other departments, but now you LOST the streamlined ease and basic functionlity that QuickBooks offers. Maybe you should consider having SEPERATE software applications to keep track of what it is you are trying to incorporate into your accounting system. Or use a company like Level Field which can lay on top of all of your software applications (CRM, ERP, accounting, marketing, etc) in real time. Talk to my friend Jude Samson to learn more - http://www.levelfield.com.

column7: QuickBooks Desktop Pro (QBD) is powerful and full-featured. Several of the main features (bill and invoice generation, invoice numbering, etc) can be automated, which helps with productivity. Once you learn how to translate your context into QB activities, it's not quite as user-hostile.
column8: It's unintuitive unless you're a trained bookkeeper or accountant, and it will eat your life if you try to make sense of it unassisted. Do NOT go there. Most candid experts say it's designed to permit accounting pros a suitable level of flexibility while alienating casual users -- in that mission, it definitely succeeds. There's no documentation and the crowd-sourced knowledgebase is pathetic and poorly supported, so without a trained expert to help, you're entirely on your own.
column9: I use it for my accounting, spending 10-15 hours a week with it. You need to concentrate while using it; there's nothing innately helpful or supportive about QBD that will prevent you from making expensive, hard-to-fix mistakes. It's a big-league tool that marks you as a serious business, and may pay dividends later (like when used with Intuit's TurboTax Business). Just don't count on instant gratification.
column10:
Each business is different and will use QBD differently. It's the reference standard for bankers (ie, lenders), investors, and accountants. The supplied templates for setting up your business are next-to-useless and there's no doc, so unless you're willing to invest (10-20x the cost of the product) in an expert to guide you, it's dangerous to pretend it'll help you much. I've tested every fiber of willpower mastering it because I want to be proud of my financial records, but even after 3+ years' dedicated effort its ROI is negligible.

All I know for sure is that the alternate product, toward which Intuit and some bookkeepers will push you, is QuickBooks Online and it, by comparison, is a toy -- far fewer features, maddening usability issues, and even worse knowledgebase support -- so if you don't need concurrent multi-user support or access by Mac users, stick with QB Desktop and invest (a lot) in learning to make it serve you.

column7: Quickbooks accounting software is the most user-friendly accounting software for small businesses. I have used numerous software in the accounting industry and none hit the price point and features that Quickbooks does. The ease of use and tutorials provided make this an easy product for the small business start-up, a mid-level company of revenues in the 1mil-2mil range. I have used premier, pro, and enterprise and PRO are great for small businesses without a lot of transactions. The cost is user-friendly if you do not add on any services such as payroll, etc.
column8: Quickbooks has a lot of internal promotion for the other products it offers. It can be annoying when you do not need another service, but that is what they keep trying to push at you. Quickbooks desktop pro also comes out annually now and encourages you to buy year after year, despite very few overall changes. At $300 as an average price, this can add up. They also do not offer service or help on versions older than 3 years forcing you to upgrade your version. column7:
We use quickbooks because it nominally allows us to keep track of time slips per employee for each customer based on service items. However, much of the functionality is augmented through the use of 2 different integrations because either quickbooks doesn't offer that feature at all, or charges exorbitant license fees for a whole new desktop solution when all I need is one small feature. 

That said, once you get used to how quickbooks just "does things" - using it isn't very difficult and pulling reports, etc makes a lot of sense.
column8: I don't like their lock-in business model, making it nearly impossible to switch to a different accounting solution without a tremendous amount of administrative overhead and third-party expenses. I also dislike their model of trying to get accounting done on their cloud solution, which is not at all what we would be interested in due to serious privacy concerns (like an IRS audit).
column9: Through some creative integrations, we're now able to pull up job costing reports based on employee effort logged aggregated with job cost expenses, etc. We're also able to have multiple employees log time slips through another provider which then syncs just the slip data directly into quickbooks.
column10: Think long and hard about making sure this solves ALL of your needs upfront. You may end up like us and not realizing that a feature we need has to be paid for either as a subscription fee or as a new license.

column7: Quickbooks makes creating, filing, and paying invoices extremely easy. We frequently use the statements feature to let our customers know their billing status, past due totals, and YTD totals. QuickBooks Desktop Pro also makes payroll processing a cinch. I appreciate that QuickBooks calculates required taxes and other payroll deductions. It is extremely easy to find the totals that need to be paid out to unions or 401K programs using payroll report features and filters. It also makes paying weekly 940s and 941s quick and easy.
column8: I dislike that QuickBooks can be a bit difficult to learn. It requires a bit of pre-existing knowledge and skill to find and set particular filters to find site specific jobs, addresses, and PO #s. The biggest issue we had with QuickBooks was setting up two users on the same company file on two different computers. It took awhile to set up firewalls that would allow us to both access the file, without compromising safety of our data.
column10: We are currently solving issues with vendors with significantly past due invoices. QuickBooks allows us to send monthly aging statements to each vendor. These aging statements list the invoice date, due date, and how many days past due. We are also using QuickBooks to help our company go all digital and become paperless. This saves us money and reduces the amount of waste we put into the environment. QuickBooks allows us to scan documents to attach to invoices, that we can send out to customers directly from the current screen.

column7: I like how user friendly QuickBooks is for the small-business owner. There are a lot of wizards and walk-throughs that help you set things up, and consider things that you might have forgotten about without it. The transactions are fool proof, and make the correct double entries without too much effort or thought on the users part. The reports are easy to create and customize. When it's time to do my monthly reports I am able to easily create and modify them to suit my needs. It works well for consolidating information between entities.
column8: It seems to take a lot of processing power from the computer, so if you have a slower computer, or any kind of slowness issues with your computer, QuickBooks can stop working, and periodically needs to be forcefully shut down and restarted.
column9: Every business needs a good set of books, and QuickBooks helps accomplish this basic of all business needs. It allows for the versatility to do as much or as little as you may need. Anything from balancing a check book to billing, to financial reporting, to processing your payroll.
column10: This is the best choice for a small business, even for those that have never used any accounting software, or don't know a lot about accounting in general. Make sure your computer has a good amount of RAM and a faster processor if possible as the program can take a lot of processing power.

column7: I use Quickbooks mostly for creating, processing, and tracking purchase orders and inventory for my company. I had no experience with Quickbooks a year ago, but already feel quite confident in it. Very user friendly and organized in a way that makes sense. I appreciate the way it stores data for each product number you save, and will auto populate that into future purchase orders. Also a nice feature of Quickbooks is that you can easily track PO's by date, vendor, or PO number in the program. I can check within minutes if the accountant has processed payment on a specific order, what items were submitted with a certain PO, or when the PO was submitted.
column8: While quickbooks is good at auto populating data into PO's, it sometimes is frustrating trying to edit the price, or item description, takes several steps to go in, find the right product number, and then change the data to reflect updated price/item specifications, etc. Also a bit difficult that there is no easy way to break down an item into price brackets. We deal with a lot of products that have varying prices depending on quantity ordered, but quickbooks doesn't have an easy way to input this in the system for PO creating and item descriptions.
column10: Quickbooks helps us stay organized and have detailed info on everything that is going in and coming out of our business. Quickbooks also allows for better commmunication between different departments, as multiple department heads can access the system. I deal more with order tracking and fullfillment, so if a vendor contacts me regarding a previous order we placed, or I need to process a return, everything is organized and readily available to me. This makes it easier for me to communicate with accounting, and make sure everyone knows what's going on with each order.

column7: Quickbooks desktop version has everything a company needs to maintain their financial information, produce financial reports and make well informed financial decisions. The multiple windows option allows for multitasking in reviewing different areas of accounting at once. Automated functions and renaming rules allow for fast and efficient tracking. User controls can provide the ability to delegate certain activities to other team members. Accountant copy options allow for users to continue to work in the file while the accountant is making changes and importing the accountant changes at a later date without loss of interim data. The customer and vendor centers allow for easy snapshot overviews and the initial snapshot page reminds you what to pay or collect at a glance as well as current account balances for an at a glance funds available. Budgeting feature allows for budgets per class and multiple budgets creation and tracking. Budget performance reports are very useful. Job cost and income tracking is another incredible feature as it allows transactions to be selected as billable to pre-fill your invoice for the customer when generated reducing the necessity of redundant data entry. This is also an advantage of the time sheets function in billing. Inventory control is also a breeze allowing purchase orders to be converted to bills and payments within a matter of minutes and automatically updating inventory items within this feature. Inventory reports are also very useful when placing re-orders.
column8: Quickbooks desktop is not as portable, meaning to access from different locations would require either a laptop or a remote login service.
column9: Efficiency and productivity within the accounting system is at its best with Quickbooks desktop version allowing for speedy transaction entry and navigation throughout.
column10: Highly recommend Quickbooks desktop version for owners of small to medium size businesses who need complete and accurate accounting systems while remaining efficient and productive.

We switched to QB online from Desktop and then learned the IFF file upload is not available so we had to switch back to desktop. I really liked the look and feel of the import view in the QB online and wish the Desktop version had this easy to use look and feel as well.
column10: I have not seen any significant updates or improvements in Quickbooks Desktop over the past few years of using this product. The ease with which our CPAs can access information and help with end of year reporting is also a great feature.
column8: Not super intuitive. I've been spoiled by other software and apps where the user experience is so simple I rarely have to research how to do something. column7:

The software is quite popular so it is easy to find bookkeepers and accountants familiar with this program. It also offers quite a few options for customization.
Another think I live about this program is the "Collection" module. The fact that you can create and print letters from the software is really useful.

column8: There really is not much that I do not like about quickbooks. The one main thing is when looking at individual invoices in my sales history there is no button to advance to the next invoice. column9: Quickbooks desktop helps me know where my money is going, where it is coming in from, what items are my best sellers and even what time of day we have our most sales. This has helped me to track and adjust my staffing needs saving money from staffing when we are traditionally slow and adding more staff when I know we usually do most of our sales. This has saved me dollars in salary and lost production. It has also helped with sales as there are associates here to help when we are busier instead of a customer having to wait while an associate is working with a different customer.
column10: If you are a small business owner do yourself a favor and get quickbooks. The little time that it takes to get it set up and customized is so worth it to your business. It will help you keep track of where your money comes from, goes to, how much profit you are making and even who does the most sales. The upfront cost is really nothing compared with the benefits and piece of mind you can have by using QuickBooks desktop.

column7: QB practically invented this software category a generation back, and saved millions or tens of millions of growing businesses from the ritual ignominy of storing receipts in a shoebox until a bookkeeper could mix them up. Because of QB's simple, intuitive interface, it is an easy-to-use, easy-to-learn resource designed to scale up functionality as the user's business grows and becomes more sophisticated.
column8: There's an obviousness to QB's 20th-century roots. This is not necessarily a state-of-the-art app that takes full advantage of mobile capabilities and cloud enhancements. However, what you give up in lost glitz and trendiness, you make up for in efficiency, so the product's shortcoming may actually be an asset.
column9: QB has allowed us to regularize our bookkeeping and accounting functions and enabled our key staff to focus on revenue-producing activities -- rather than the obsessive and painstaking work of keeping track of receipts, expenses, and income. We could not operate as effectively as we do without having QB as part of our infrastructure.
column10: A solid choice, proven in millions of worldwide installations.

column9: We run a consulting business and often hire contract workers whom we pay using QB desktop. Additionally, we work with several new customers and vendors each year and invoice them using QB. It has been a great tool to be able to forecast profits and budget year after year.
column10: I would highly recommend QB desktop if you are looking to bundle all of your accounting needs into one tool, Not only can you track all your customers and vendors, pay all of your employees and contractors, you can even link the product to your bank for up to the minute recording of profits and losses. column7: Ease of use and functionality. I like how all of the most important items are on the home screen for easy navigation. I like how user friendly QB is and the help menu is actually useful. I love the business reports i am able to create as well, especially my company snapshot.
column8: I would love to be able to use an app on my phone to enter my time (for single time slip) that would send to my time in Quickbooks. I would also love the ability to only "reprint" invoices or that QB would automatically save a pdf copy for me. I do not like how the invoices pick up and use the last used invoice. I would like to be able to freely move line items in an invoice and would like to be able to reflect on an invoice amount of current bill, less payments, balance due (only that particular job) without the hassle of creating a new layout.
column9: I used this software when working for attorneys in law firms and it was awesome. I used it for payroll, payables and receivables. I love being able to review my company snapshot to not only see company growth but to track spending. Awesome Software, Easy Navigation and One

column7: Quickbooks Desktop is an old standby. It's what I learned bookkeeping on, so it's the most familiar and easy to use. I use it to maintain books for multiple companies. For most companies, it does everything I need it to do. It handles bank feeds well, as well as imports and exports of data.
column8:
I have a client who uses Intuit GoPayments. Although this product is advertised as working seamlessly with Quickbooks Desktop, this is not the case. Transactions are frequently missing and Quickbooks Desktop requires that each and every transaction have an invoice created for it. I do not need invoices, because the payments are made at the time that service is rendered. This is not the case with Quickbooks Online - on QBO, you do not have to create invoices and Intuit GoPayments plays quite nicely with QBO - you can just click to issue a sales receipt.

Unfortunately, this isn't the first time that a feature that exists on Quickbooks Online has been missing from the Desktop version. I have concluded that Intuit is making an effort to move away from the Desktop version in order to focus their efforts on QBO.
column9: I use the Accountant version of QB Desktop and I use it to manage books for several clients who do not have any accounting software. All of my clients appreciate the reports that Quickbooks Desktop can produce, and data is easy to export to Excel to manipulate and analyze. Quickbooks
column10: My recommendation is to take a hard look at Quickbooks Online and see if it suits their needs. I feel that Quickbooks Desktop is a form of the software that is losing support from Intuit and destined to go out of date.

column7: This is an affordable accounting program that can handle most accounting functions easily. The payroll module is simple to use and handles all quarterly and annual reports. The Sales Tax module is very helpful. A/R and A/P are simple to use. The banking downloads and reconciliations are very simple and save time. The reports are customizable and user friendly. The inventory module is very comprehensive and integrates with the general ledger easily. The job costing function is also very simple to use and provides detailed reports for each job or project.
column8: It is not easy to enter inter-company transactions and consolidate financials for more than one entity. You must set up a separate file for each entity and enter transactions in each file, then download into Excel and consolidate manually.
column9: A/R, A/P and Payroll are simple to use. Downloading bank transactions and reconciling accounts saves time and is very simple. Tracking sales tax is simple as well as filing payroll tax returns and year end payroll tax forms.
column10: Most accounting functions can be handled easily. However, if you have more than one entity and post inter-company transactions and need consolidated financials, this will not be easy. The payroll module is very easy to use and provides quarterly and annual tax reports.

column8: Quickbooks (Intuit) sucks at making their product integrate well with other products. Take Salesforce for example. column7: QuickBook payroll is extremely intuitive and simple to use. It makes completing our semi-monthly salary employees as well as hourly payroll easy to enter and process. I would recommend QuickBooks payroll to anyone with a small to medium sized business. QuickBooks makes it easy and convenient to submit tax payments as well. The time tracking and PTO tracking options are great as well. It makes it easy to keep track of all out employee information as well. I am able to set up an new employee including their banking information for direct deposits in the matter of minutes. When using a payroll company, it takes several days to verify all the information and there is almost always mistakes made. When using QuickBooks I can enter in all the info manually and it doesn't take any time at all.
column8: When mistakes are made there are sometimes several steps involved to correct them, other than that, that is the only thing that I dislike.
column9: When using payroll via QuickBooks, it saves time and money within a business. I don't need to rely on someone else to handle the payroll and I am able to complete it easily and with out any issues. Also, QuickBooks payroll is compatible with Excel and its is easy to upload payroll information from Excel documents.
column10: QuickBooks is simple and easy to use and very intuitive.

column7: I like that Intuit has added grid-lines that were not there in the 2014 edition. I also like the intuitiveness of the accounts receivable and accounts payable options.
column8:
I dislike the inability of the Bank Reconciliation Detail Reports to be updated (e.g. when I enter a Paycheck and need to delete the original regular Check, the Bank Reconciliation Detail Report does not allow me to update or produce another report with the updated Paycheck).

I dislike that the 1099MISC issuance sync takes too long. I've wasted a tremendous amount of time during tax season because of this issue.
column10: The major benefit I've realize is the ability of the software to recognize Paychecks when downloading transactions via the Bank Feed. QuickBooks Premier - Accountant

column7: I was born and raised on Quickbooks so I don't have any experience using any other programs but I like the reports in Quickbooks. It is fairly easy to learn and a good standard in house book keeping program.

column9: IN house accounting and book keeping. Sales reports. Register tracking. We prefer to do our own in house book keeping and then send the reconciled statements and reports to the accountant for our taxes. For one, it forces us to be very aware of our bottom line and sales. It also gives us a way to monitor our sales for tax purposes so we aren't too surprised when the tax bill comes.
column10: I have only used this program in various updates for the last 12 years. It might not be the best, but Intuit has a lot of years of experience behind them and I trust that knowledge base.

QuickBooks

column10: Quickbooks offers both a desktop version and an online version. Having used both versions, each has it's pluses and minuses. If you need the ability to access your Quickbooks data from anywhere, then it is best to consider the online version. The online version will give you the functionality of accessing from anywhere but has limitations. Explore the limitations with regard to what your needs are before you commit. If you need anywhere access but can't use the online version, the other option is deploying your desktop to the cloud. There are companies who will host your desktop software and data in the cloud to allow for anywhere access. This option is, however, much more expensive than the online version.

column7: I knew nothing about QB going into my last job and no one there could really teach me. When processing payroll or entering accounts receiveables/payables, I had to figure it out on my own. A couple google searches and I was a pro in no time at all. I very much liked the ease of use.
column8: When running accrual reports, sometimes I would forget to change the months and that through off all my reports. column10: QB really helped our business run smoothly. It was easy to see past due invoices we were waiting on so I could reach out to clients to get payment. QB made our bank reconciliations so easy. Without QB, I'm not sure how well we would have stayed on top of all our accounting.

column9:
Importing Excel documents has been a life saver, especially for the first time user who has kept his records on a spreadsheet. When small businesses call me mid year (or later) to say they need to have records by the end of the year, I get data from the bank that is too old to be available online, but is available in Excel format. As a contract bookkeeper/accountant, I can actually make money and not charge the client unaffordable fees by using the automated entry tasks. column7: I use QuickBooks for about twelve years and like it for its features. This is the third accounting software I am using.
column8: Not all menus there are intuitive, especially for light users. A few times I called the tech support as I could not find how to make some changes. Each time I spent about half an hour on the phone arguing with the person on the other side trying to charge me for this technical support. My point always has been that after I paid for the software, if I have difficulties with using it on my own, the tech support should be free. After spending about half an hour on the phone, I am usually able to convince the tech support to give me free support, as I threat them that I will keep them on the phone endlessly. The last time, the actual support was: “click on (this) tab, then select this (item) from the roll down menu, and this is where the desired changes can be made.” Ten seconds the most. But they did not do it at the first place. They tried to extort money from me, not for actual work done, but because of the flaws in their design I had difficulties in finding the desired function. In my eyes, this justifies looking for alternatives.

column10: There is a learning curve in finding out how to use this software; especially, if someone does not have at least basic knowledge of accounting.
